name: Voyager Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Voyager Therapeutics is a biotechnology company developing genetic medicines for neurological diseases, focusing on Alzheimer's disease, Friedreich's ataxia, Parkinson's disease, and other CNS disorders. Its wholly-owned pipeline includes two tau-targeting programs: VY1706, a tau silencing gene therapy, and VY7523, an anti-tau antibody. The company also advances partnered programs with collaborators including Neurocrine, Novartis, and Transition Bio, leveraging its TRACER AAV capsid discovery and Voyager NeuroShuttle non-viral delivery platforms.
sellsTo: Patients with neurological diseases (indirect through partners)

exposures.sentence: We are a biotechnology company whose mission is to leverage the power of human genetics to modify the course of and ultimately cure neurological diseases.

catalysts.event: Complete GLP toxicology study for VY1706
catalysts.sentence: We expect to complete a good laboratory practices, or GLP, toxicology study in the first quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Type C communication with FDA for VY1706
catalysts.sentence: Having had a Type C communication with the FDA in the first quarter of 2026, we anticipate the submission of an investigational new drug, or IND, application in the second quarter of 2026 and initiation of a clinical trial for the VY1706 program in the second half of 2026.
we anticipate the submission of an investigational new drug, or IND, application in the second quarter of 2026
initiation of a clinical trial for the VY1706 program in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 1 MAD trial for VY7523
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we initiated a Phase 1 multiple ascending dose, or MAD, clinical trial of VY7523 in early AD patients.

catalysts.event: Initial tau PET imaging data for VY7523
catalysts.sentence: We expect initial tau positron emission tomography, or PET, imaging data in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Neurocrine initiates FA clinical trial with NBIB-223
catalysts.sentence: Neurocrine has stated that, pending successful FDA IND clearance, it intends to initiate a clinical trial with NBIB-223 for FA in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Alzheimer's disease estimated to affect over 7 million older adults in the US (2025) and up to 416 million globally (2022).
tamClaims.sentence: Alzheimer's disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disease estimated to affect over 7 million older adults in the United States (as of 2025) and up to 416 million older adults globally (as of 2022).

tamClaims.claim: Friedreich's ataxia affects approximately 5,000 patients in the US.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Friedreich's Ataxia Research Alliance, there are approximately 5,000 patients living with the disease in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Parkinson's disease affects about one million patients in the US and more than 10.0 million worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: PD is among the most common neurodegenerative diseases, affecting about one million patients in the United States and more than 10.0 million patients worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: Gaucher disease affects approximately 6,000 patients in the US.
tamClaims.sentence: Gaucher disease affects approximately 6,000 patients in the U.S.
